uploadedFiles.title: Uploaded certificate files (advanced)
uploadedFiles.description: Files currently stored under the BYO cert directory. Each row corresponds to one folder on disk. Orphans (rows without a matching Certificate entry above) accumulate when you delete a Certificate entry without removing its PEM, or when files are dropped in by hand. Cleanup here removes the on-disk PEM only — Certificate entries in state are unaffected.

confirmDelete.title: Delete uploaded files?
confirmDelete.bodySuffix: from disk? Any Certificate entry pointing at this name will become un-loadable until you replace the files.

providerCell.linkTitle: Jump to this TLS provider on the Providers tab

page.title: Certificates
page.forceRenewAllTitle: Renew all even if existing certs aren't near expiry (--force-renewal)
page.description: Adding/editing/deleting certificates changes state but does not by itself issue or revoke anything. Use Renew (per cert or all) to invoke certbot. Renewal can take several minutes per certificate due to DNS propagation.
page.forceIssueTitle: Force issue {{name}}, ignoring certbot's renewal interval
page.forceRenewTitle: Force renew {{name}}, ignoring certbot's renewal interval
page.cloneTitle: Duplicate this certificate entry with a new id/certName. Useful for splitting one cert into per-host certs, or for staging a renewal against a fresh certName. Edit the SAN list before issuing.

confirmDelete.title: Delete certificate?
confirmDelete.suffix: from state? The PEM files on disk remain until pruned by the next render pass (or removed manually from the Uploaded files panel below).

tlsProviders.title: TLS / ACME providers
tlsProviders.description: Certbot challenge providers (DNS-01 per registrar, HTTP-01, BYO). Certificates pick one of these to satisfy the ACME challenge. Credentials are stored as mode-600 files in patchpanel's credentials directory — the per-provider form below collects them.
tlsProviders.referencedTitle: Referenced by at least one certificate.

confirmDelete.title: Delete TLS provider?
confirmDelete.suffix: ? The stored credentials file (if any) is also removed from disk.

field.idHelp: Stable identifier. Certs reference accounts by id.

acmeAccount.eabHint: {{server}} requires External Account Binding. Generate the key ID + HMAC pair in the CA dashboard and paste them above before saving.

section.title: ACME accounts
section.description: Each account = an email registered with one ACME CA. Multiple accounts let you split certs across CAs, separate rate-limit pools, or run staging + prod side-by-side.

acmeAccount.emptyState: No ACME accounts defined. Every non-BYO cert needs one — add at least one before issuing.
acmeAccount.cannotDelete: Cannot delete: {{count}} cert(s) still reference this account

validate.idFormat: id must match a-z, 0-9, _, - (starting with a letter)
validate.emailRequired: email is required and must be valid
validate.directoryUrlRequired: directoryUrl is required when server is "custom"
validate.eabRequired: {{server}} requires External Account Binding — set both eabKid and eabHmacKey
validate.eabPair: eabKid and eabHmacKey must be set together (or both empty)
validate.tupleTaken: another account already uses email "{{email}}" on server "{{server}}"

certEdit.pickProviderHint: Pick a TLS provider above to continue.

providerSelect.label: TLS provider
providerSelect.locked: Provider is locked once a cert is created.

acme.certName: certName (used as Let's Encrypt cert name)
acme.domainsHelp: The first entry becomes the certificate's Common Name. Wildcards are permitted (DNS-01 only).
acme.expanding: Allow expanding existing lineage with new SANs (--expand)

validate.idFormat: id must match a-z, 0-9, _, - (starting with a letter)
validate.certNameInvalid: certName must be a valid filesystem-safe certificate name
validate.domainRequired: at least one domain is required

new.hint: Paste in your fullchain + private key, give it a name, click Validate then Upload. patchpanel writes the PEM files to disk and adds the matching Certificate entry — no need to wire up a TLS provider separately.
new.name: Name
new.namePlaceholder: auto-filled from the cert's first SAN
new.nameHelp: Used as the on-disk folder name and the entry name in the certificates list. Filesystem-safe characters only. Auto-filled from the cert's first SAN after validation; override before uploading if you want.

edit.replaceWarning: Paste new fullchain + private key below, click Validate, then Replace. The old PEM is overwritten on disk; if the new cert has different SANs the cert entry's domains are updated to match.
edit.certNameHelp: The folder name on disk. Renaming after upload is not recommended — the existing PEM files stay under the original name.
edit.domainsHelp: Auto-derived from the cert's SANs at upload time. Edits here only change what HAProxy advertises for SNI; they don't change the actual PEM bytes.

pickCert.label: Which certificate are you troubleshooting?
pickCert.empty: No certificates in state yet. Use the cert wizard first.

preflight.goBack: Go back and pick a cert.

action.label: What do you want to do?

renew.title: Renew if needed.
renew.desc: Standard renewal — certbot decides based on remaining lifetime.

force.title: Force renew.
force.desc: Re-issues even if the current cert isn't close to expiry. Use after switching staging↔prod or after credentials changed.

skip.title: Skip renewal.
skip.desc: Close the wizard without changing anything.

run.inProgress: Renewal in progress. DNS-01 propagation can take a couple minutes per cert — leave the wizard open.
run.reloadFailed: Renewal completed but HAProxy reload failed.

letsencrypt.description: Global renewal settings. Account identity (email + ACME server) lives per-account in the ACME accounts card above. Each certificate picks which account it uses.

credentials.preservedHint: Existing value preserved on save. Clear and retype to replace.

tlsProviderEdit.templateUnavailable: Credentials template unavailable: {{message}}. You can still save the provider; add credentials later via Edit.
tlsProviderEdit.idHelp: Lowercase a-z, digits, _ or -. Used as the on-disk credentials filename.
tlsProviderEdit.typeHelp: Type cannot change after creation. Delete and recreate to switch.
tlsProviderEdit.noCredentials: This provider type does not use a credentials file.

validate.idFormat: id must match a-z, 0-9, _, - (starting with a letter)

upload.title: Upload trusted CA bundle
upload.pemHelpPrefix: Paste or drop a PEM bundle. Multiple
upload.subject: Subject:
upload.descPrefix: A trusted CA is a PEM bundle (root + optional intermediates) that HAProxy uses to verify peer certificates. Use it on a
upload.descMiddle: for mTLS client cert validation, or on a
upload.descSuffix: to validate an upstream's TLS chain.
upload.idHelpPrefix: Stable identifier; used in state refs and as the on-disk filename. Lowercase a-z, 0-9,

list.title: Trusted CAs
list.descPrefix: CA bundles used by HAProxy to verify peer certificates. Referenced from frontend bind
list.empty: No trusted CAs uploaded yet. Add one to enable mTLS client validation on binds or upstream TLS verification on backend servers.
list.cannotDelete: Cannot delete: still referenced by {{binds}} bind(s) and {{servers}} server(s)
list.stillReferencedTitle: Still referenced by {{binds}} bind(s) and {{servers}} server(s)

upload.title: Upload trusted CRL
upload.pemHelpPrefix: Paste or drop a PEM-encoded X.509 Certificate Revocation List. HAProxy validates the file contents at
upload.pemHelpSuffix: time; patchpanel only checks the PEM envelope here.
upload.descPrefix: A Certificate Revocation List is a signed list of certificates that have been revoked by their issuing CA. HAProxy uses it on a
upload.descSuffix: + a Trusted CA to reject revoked client certs at the TLS handshake.
upload.idHelpPrefix: Stable id; on-disk filename. Lowercase a-z, 0-9,

list.title: Trusted CRLs
list.descPrefix: X.509 Certificate Revocation Lists. Referenced from frontend bind
list.descSuffix: when doing mTLS client cert validation — HAProxy uses the CRL to reject revoked client certs at the TLS handshake.
list.empty: No trusted CRLs uploaded yet. Upload one to gate mTLS access via revocation status.
list.cannotDelete: Cannot delete: still referenced by {{count}} bind(s)
